# Creation Automation Tiers

**Flexibility: Suggested (MAY)**

## The Standard

Creation workflows MAY be organized into three maturity tiers:

| Tier | Name | Characteristics | When to Use |
|------|------|-----------------|-------------|
| 1 | Guide | Documentation only. Human/agent reads and follows. | Rare, creative, or low-volume creation |
| 2 | Scaffolder | Deterministic automation. No AI, no judgment. | High-volume mechanical creation |
| 3 | Engine | Intelligent automation. AI-assisted, human checkpoints. | Creation requiring research or judgment |

**Rules:**
- Every creation workflow starts as a Guide (Tier 1). Guides are the source of truth.
- Scaffolders automate Guides mechanically. They don't replace Guides — they implement them.
- Engines add intelligence on top. They may call Scaffolders for mechanical steps.
- The correct tier depends on creation complexity, not organizational preference.

## What Good Looks Like

- Every creation type has at least a Guide.
- High-volume creation types have Scaffolders that produce governance-compliant artifacts.
- Complex creation types with significant blast radius have Engines with human checkpoints.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-C17 (Graduated Automation) — the concept behind this standard
- JOS-C09 (Cynefin Framework) — domain complexity determines the appropriate tier
